text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic nascent fundoplication for hiatus hernia repair, initiating with a 12mm OptiView entry following pneumoperitoneum. A diagnostic laparoscopy is conducted, and two 5mm ports are placed as working ports. An additional 5mm port is positioned on the left side for gastric retraction. Liver retraction is achieved using a 3mm Nathanson retractor.

The patient is positioned head-up, and the stomach is emptied. The surgeon begins gastrolysis on the stomach, carefully dissecting the short gastric vessels up to the phrenoesophageal membrane. The gastrohepatic ligament is opened to identify the right crus, ensuring the meaty fibers remain covered by a fat sheet. The Belsey pad of fat is dissected off the anterior esophagus near the crura. Retro-esophageal dissection is performed, with an attempt to enter the thorax for mobilization. The left crus is also dissected, keeping the meaty fibers covered. A 12-inch umbilical tape is passed retro-esophageally to retract the esophagus upward.

The surgeon uses a harmonic device to free or dissect all attachments around the esophagus, ensuring the posterior vagus nerve remains close to the esophagus. After complete mobilization, crurography is performed using polyester number two sutures with a 45 mm taper point needle, carefully placing two to three sutures to close the crural defect without injuring the liver, stomach, or esophagus. The adequacy of the closure is checked using a gastric calibration tube.

The surgeon creates a gastro-gastric wrap by taking a stay suture on the posterior wall of the stomach at the fundus, passing the stomach retro-esophageal. The shoe shine maneuver is performed to ensure no twisting of the stomach walls. The wrap is initiated with 2-0 polyester gastro-gastric sutures, including the anterior wall of the esophagus in the first suture to prevent slippage. The right side of the stomach is fixed to the right crus for cruropexy to prevent migration. The wrap is completed with three or four 2-0 polyester sutures, forming a 3.5 cm wrap. The Nathanson port and all other ports are removed under vision, and no drain is left.
